title: NATLClaw External Persona Manifest
description: Standalone persona.json manifest for a persona developed in a 3rd-party repository. Place this file in the persona directory and point to the directory via personasPaths in the host mcp.json.

name.description: Persona identifier. Defaults to the directory name if omitted.

description.description: Short human-readable label for the persona.

instructions.description: Path to the markdown system-prompt file. Relative paths resolve from this persona.json's directory. Defaults to 'instructions.md'.

heartbeatTask.description: Prompt injected during the capture step. Required for second_brain and freeform; optional for steps.

workflow.description: Heartbeat workflow mode. second_brain (default): structured JSON knowledge capture. freeform: free-form execution with tool use, plain-text note. steps: fully custom steps array.

prompt.description: Use {prev} for previous step output, {brain} for brain summary.

stepwise.description: When true (and workflow=steps), run exactly ONE step per heartbeat. Progress persists across cycles via agent state. Defaults to false (all steps run each heartbeat).

mcpServers.description: Names of MCP servers to attach. These must be defined in the host mcp.json mcpServers pool — the persona does not define servers itself.

dream.description: Idle dream-cycle policy for background memory maintenance.

enabled.description: Enable automatic scheduler dream runs during idle periods.

idleStreakMin.description: Number of consecutive idle heartbeats required before a dream run.

maxAgeDays.description: Stale-note decay threshold used by dream pruning.

tools.description: Python tool configuration. Use 'file' for a local .py file (recommended for 3rd-party repos) or 'module' for an installed package.

file.description: Path to a .py file containing tool functions. Relative to this persona.json. Use this when the persona is not an installed Python package.

module.description: Dotted Python module path (e.g. 'my_persona_pkg.tools'). The package must be installed in the agent's Python environment.

functions.description: Specific function names to expose as tools. If omitted, all public functions in the file/module are used.
